task_signature.context: Implement a v2/ screen refactor following CLAUDE.md v2 convention.

learned_patterns.decision: 4 steps: (1) create {parent}/v2/ directory, (2) create {parent}/v2/{SameFilename}.swift with same Swift type name as v1, (3) in pbxproj: add PBXGroup for v2/, add PBXFileReference+PBXBuildFile for v2 file, REMOVE v1 from Sources build phase (keep v1 as PBXFileReference for git history), (4) add HISTORICAL header comment to v1 file.
When a v2 file exceeds ~400 lines, extract sub-views into the same v2/ directory as separate files. Training v2 extracted 6 sub-view files from a 2,135-line v1 screen.
One commit for v2 file + pbxproj swap together. Separate commits for analytics enum additions, test files, and docs/state updates. Mixing these into one commit makes review and revert harder.

anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Onboarding v2 used 20+ patch commits on the original v1 file, making the diff hard to review and the historical baseline unclear. Always create a fresh v2 file in v2/ subdirectory.
Onboarding v2 required 3 follow-up fix commits because the pbxproj was updated separately. The build breaks between those commits. Always update pbxproj in the same commit as the v2 file creation.

Reading v1 first and copying its structure results in re-introducing its anti-patterns. Build bottom-up from AppTheme tokens and AppComponents, then assemble the screen. v1 is read-only reference for behavior spec, not structure.

speedup_instructions: Follow 4-step v2/ recipe. If v1 > 400 lines, plan extracted views. Update pbxproj in same commit. Build bottom-up from AppTheme+AppComponents.
